project_type: Python + MQL5 EA trading system (NeuroX v9 Pure Momentum HF Scalper)
build_system: pip for Python deps, Start.bat for full compile+launch (Windows-only)
test_framework: pytest with tests/ directory containing test_bridge.py, test_momentum.py, test_tick_collector.py
verification_instructions: 1. Install deps: pip install numpy pandas pytest. 2. Run pytest tests/ -v. 3. Verify no import errors in main.py, bridge.py, momentum.py, tick_collector.py, trailing_stop.py.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. No MT5 available for live testing. Python-only validation via pytest. MQL5 code cannot be compiled (no MetaEditor in sandbox).
contribution_requirements: Push to a feature branch, create PR against neurox-v9-release.
key_patterns: Architecture: Python computes momentum from tick prices, writes signals to MT5 Common Files via bridge.py (flag-based IPC), EA reads signals on OnTick and executes. Signal prefix: neurox_v9_. Magic: 20250629. Config in config.py. Trailing stop tiers in both Python (trailing_stop.py) and EA (NeuroX_Position.mqh). EA already enforces max_trades=5 via InpMaxOpenTrades. The confirmation timeout is cosmetic since EA executes fine without writing back.
directory_structure: Root: Python files (main.py, bridge.py, config.py, momentum.py, tick_collector.py, trailing_stop.py). Include/: MQL5 header files for EA. tests/: pytest test files. NeuroX_EA_v9.mq5: main EA file.
